texto: In the days before computers and emails,” she explained, “people used to write messages on paper and send them to each other using a fax machine. It’s sort of like sending a photograph through a telephone line. Hardly anyone uses a fax these days. They’re very old fashioned,” she continued, “but they used to be very popular once upon a time. Lots of little children used to send their letters to Santa that way.” "Wow!” said Poppy Noodle. “That sounds like Magic.” “No,” answered Flora sharply. “That’s not Magic, that’s technology. Never confuse the two.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Harald Davidson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “In the days before computers and emails,” she explained, “people used to write messages on paper …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, funny-quotes, kids pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e kids.
